_note: ROUND 3 (great-grandparents) for batch e_1400_1750_1 (early-modern round-2 grandparents, 1400-1750). For each round-2 node in the batch, its documented parents are added one generation further back (new nodes use the R3H_ prefix). Filled 31 of 34 batch ids across 30 families (Elizabeth Cheney occupies ONE family for her two duplicate ids). Skipped 3: R2L_JOHNSAY (Sir John Say's parentage is unconfirmed/disputed — competing Podington-Saye, Heron, and Fiennes theories, none documented), R2L_ALICE_STOURTONW (only 'Alice', 2nd wife of the elder John Stourton; her own parentage undocumented), and R2L_JOHNSTOURTON_D1438 (see DEDUP/DATA-ERROR flag below). MAJOR DEDUP / CROSS-LINKS: (1) Antoine of Vaudémont's father Ferry I of Lorraine, Count of Vaudémont, is added as an ADDITIONAL documented child of the EXISTING R2H_JOHNIDUKELORRAINE + R2H_SOPHIEWURTTEMBERG union (younger brother of the existing Charles II of Lorraine) rather than re-creating that generation; (2) Ferdinand I 'Ferrante' of Naples is the illegitimate son of the EXISTING R2I_ALFONSO5_ARAGON (Alfonso V) by his mistress Giraldona Carlino (new node) — father referenced, not duplicated; (3) Beatriz of Portugal, Duchess of Viseu, is added as an ADDITIONAL child of the EXISTING JOHNCONST (John, Constable of Portugal) + ISABBARCELOS (Isabel of Barcelos) union — full sister of the existing ISABPORT (Isabella of Portugal, Queen of Castile) — no new grandparents created; (4) John Stewart, 1st Earl of Atholl, is the son of Sir James Stewart the Black Knight of Lorne (new node) by the EXISTING JOANBEAUSCOT (Joan Beaufort, dowager Queen of Scots) — mother referenced, not duplicated; full brothers James (Earl of Buchan) and Andrew (Bishop of Moray) added. DATA-ERROR FLAG (pre-existing, not editable here): the round-2 file 41_grandparents_undated.json pairs R2L_JOHNSTOURTON_D1438 (Sir John Stourton d.1438) AS SPOUSE of R2L_ALICE_STOURTONW (Alice d.1407) and makes them parents of Edith Stourton. This is a generational conflation: historically Alice (d.1407) was the SECOND WIFE of the ELDER John Stourton of Stourton (c.1334-1406), and John (d.1438, the escheator) and Edith were both their CHILDREN (History of Parliament; soc.genealogy.medieval). Correctly adding John d.1438's parents (John Stourton the elder + Alice) would make R2L_ALICE_STOURTONW simultaneously his wife and his mother, a contradiction/cycle. Both Stourton-undated nodes are therefore SKIPPED for round 3 and the conflation flagged for a future correction pass. DISPUTE/SOURCE FLAGS: Amerigo Sanseverino's parents (Filippo Sanseverino, Count of Matera, & Elisabetta d'Aquino) and Richard Culpeper's parents (Sir William Culpeper & Elizabeth Ferrers) and Elizabeth Coker's parents (Sir Robert Coker & Joan Wallys) rest on genealogical compilations (Geneanet/Geni/WikiTree/thepeerage) rather than a dedicated Wikipedia article — sourced accordingly in each note. Web-verified July 2026 against Wikipedia (English/French) and, where flagged, genealogical databases. 👑 = reigning kings/emperors only; ✝ = clergy.

Added as an additional child of that existing union rather than re-creating the grandparents. Wikipedia.

Beatriz of Portugal, Duchess of Viseu and Beja, was daughter of the existing John, Constable of Portugal (JOHNCONST, Lord of Reguengos), & Isabel of Barcelos (ISABBARCELOS) — full sister of the existing Isabella of Portugal, Queen of Castile (ISABPORT). Added as an additional child of the existing union; no new grandparents created. Wikipedia.
